 {"id":"bd-209","title":"Implement dispatch_command()","description":"**Implement subcommand routing**

## What
Create the command dispatcher that routes to appropriate subcommand functions.

## Why
Subcommand architecture provides clean separation. The dispatcher is the central router.

## Implementation
```bash
dispatch_command() {
    local cmd="${1:-sync}"  # Default to sync
    shift || true

    case "$cmd" in
        sync)       cmd_sync "$@" ;;
        status)     cmd_status "$@" ;;
        init)       cmd_init "$@" ;;
        add)        cmd_add "$@" ;;
        list)       cmd_list "$@" ;;
        doctor)     cmd_doctor "$@" ;;
        self-update) cmd_self_update "$@" ;;
        config)     cmd_config "$@" ;;
        -h|--help)  show_help ;;
        -v|--version) show_version ;;
        *)
            # Maybe it's a file/URL passed directly
            if [[ -f "$cmd" || "$cmd" =~ ^https?:// ]]; then
                cmd_sync "$cmd" "$@"
            else
                log_error "Unknown command: $cmd"
                exit 4
            fi
            ;;
    esac
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands route correctly
- Default command is sync
- Unknown commands error with exit code 4
- Files/URLs passed directly trigger sync","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.327002117-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.327002117-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in initial ru script skeleton","labels":["cli","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-209","depends_on_id":"bd-208","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.212452531-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-210","title":"Implement parse_global_args() and main()","description":"**Implement argument parsing and main entry point**

## What
Parse global arguments (--json, --quiet, etc.) and create the main() entry point.

## Why
Global options apply to all commands and must be parsed before dispatch.

## Global Options
- --help, -h: Show help
- --version, -v: Show version
- --json: JSON output mode
- --quiet, -q: Minimal output
- --verbose: Detailed output
- --non-interactive: Never prompt
- --dry-run: Show what would happen

## Implementation Pattern
```bash
main() {
    # Parse global args first
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            --json) JSON_MODE=true; shift ;;
            --quiet|-q) QUIET_MODE=true; shift ;;
            --verbose) VERBOSE_MODE=true; shift ;;
            --non-interactive) INTERACTIVE=false; shift ;;
            --dry-run) DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
            --) shift; break ;;
            -*) break ;;  # Let dispatch handle unknown flags
            *) break ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Check gum availability
    check_gum
    
    # Dispatch to subcommand
    dispatch_command "$@"
}

main "$@"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Global args parsed before dispatch
- Flags correctly set runtime state
- Unknown args passed to subcommands
- Script runs when executed directly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.328450456-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.328450456-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in initial ru script skeleton","labels":["cli","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-210","depends_on_id":"bd-209","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.241926646-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3","title":"Phase 3: Configuration System","description":"**EPIC: XDG-Compliant Configuration System**

## Goal
Implement a configuration system that follows XDG Base Directory Specification, allowing users to customize behavior while providing sensible defaults.

## Rationale
The original plan had a critical flaw: default lists pointing to repo-local files that won't exist after installation. XDG compliance solves this elegantly - config lives in ~/.config/ru/, data in ~/.local/share/ru/, cache in ~/.cache/ru/, and state in ~/.local/state/ru/.

## Configuration Resolution Order
1. Command-line arguments (--dir, --layout)
2. Environment variables (RU_PROJECTS_DIR, RU_LAYOUT)
3. Config file (~/.config/ru/config)
4. Built-in defaults

This order is critical: CLI always wins, then env (for CI), then file (for user prefs), then defaults.

## Key Config Values
- PROJECTS_DIR: Where repos live (default: /data/projects)
- LAYOUT: flat|owner-repo|full (default: flat for backwards compat)
- UPDATE_STRATEGY: ff-only|rebase|merge (default: ff-only for safety)
- AUTOSTASH: Whether to auto-stash before pull (default: false)

## Success Criteria
- `ru init` creates config directory and files
- Config values resolve correctly through the priority chain
- User can override any value via CLI, env, or file","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:22:47.459277496-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:22:47.459277496-05:00","close_reason":"Configuration System EPIC complete - all config functions implemented","labels":["config","xdg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3","depends_on_id":"bd-2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:13:08.982275423-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-301","title":"Implement ensure_dir() utility","description":"**Create directory creation utility**

## What
A utility function that creates directories if they don't exist.

## Why
We create many directories (config, logs, cache). A utility centralizes this with consistent error handling.

## Implementation
```bash
ensure_dir() {
    local dir="$1"
    if [[ ! -d "$dir" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$dir" || {
            log_error "Failed to create directory: $dir"
            return 1
        }
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Creates directory if missing
- No-op if exists
- Returns error on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:20:58.115075257-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:20:58.115075257-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in Phase 2 skeleton","labels":["config","util"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-301","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.271271838-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-301","depends_on_id":"bd-201","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.299448879-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-503","title":"Implement gum_spin()","description":"**Spinner with fallback**

## What
Function to show a spinner during operations with fallback to simple output.

## Why
Spinners provide feedback during potentially long operations. Without gum, we show static messages.

## Implementation
```bash
gum_spin() {
    local title="$1"
    shift
    
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" && "$QUIET_MODE" != "true" ]]; then
        gum spin --spinner dot --title "$title" -- "$@"
    else
        [[ "$QUIET_MODE" != "true" ]] && echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} $title" >&2
        "$@"
    fi
}
```

## Note on Usage
Not heavily used in v1 since we show per-repo progress. May be useful for operations like 'Checking for updates...'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shows spinner with gum
- Shows static message without gum
- Executes command in both cases
- Respects quiet mode","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:55:21.097288749-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:55:21.097288749-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented gum_spin() with spinner and ANSI fallback, respects quiet mode","labels":["gum","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-503","depends_on_id":"bd-501","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.005399672-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-504","title":"Implement print_banner()","description":"**Styled banner display**

## What
Function to print the tool banner at startup.

## Why
A nice banner establishes identity and professionalism. Different styles for gum vs ANSI.

## With Gum
```
╭──────────────────────────────────────╮
│  🔄 ru v1.0.0                        │
│  Repo Updater                        │
╰──────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Without Gum (ANSI)
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ru v1.0.0 - Repo Updater
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

## Implementation
```bash
print_banner() {
    [[ "$QUIET_MODE" == "true" ]] && return
    
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        gum style --border rounded --padding "0 2" \
            "🔄 ru v$VERSION" "Repo Updater"
    else
        echo -e "${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}" >&2
        echo -e "  ${BOLD}ru${NC} v$VERSION - Repo Updater" >&2
        echo -e "${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}" >&2
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Beautiful with gum
- Acceptable without gum
- Respects quiet mode
- Goes to stderr","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:54:54.439295998-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:54:54.439295998-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented print_banner() with gum and ANSI fallback, respects quiet mode, goes to stderr","labels":["gum","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-504","depends_on_id":"bd-501","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.033374852-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-504","depends_on_id":"bd-202","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.084853619-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-505","title":"Test gum available and unavailable modes","description":"**Test both UI modes**

## What
Verify the tool works correctly with and without gum installed.

## Why
Users without gum must have full functionality. We can't assume everyone has it.

## Test Scenarios
1. With gum installed: Full beautiful output
2. Without gum: Functional ANSI fallback
3. With --quiet: Minimal output in both modes
4. Non-interactive: No prompts in either mode

## Test Method
```bash
# Test without gum
PATH_WITHOUT_GUM=$(echo $PATH | tr ':' '\n' | grep -v gum | tr '\n' ':')
PATH="$PATH_WITHOUT_GUM" ru sync --dry-run

# Test with gum
ru sync --dry-run
```

 {"id":"bd-6","title":"Phase 6: Dependency Management","description":"**EPIC: GitHub CLI Detection & Dependency Management**

## Goal
Implement robust detection and guided installation of dependencies, particularly the GitHub CLI (gh), without ever auto-installing without consent.

## Rationale
The tool needs gh for cloning private repos and API access. But automatically installing system packages is invasive and breaks trust. We detect, inform, and prompt - never auto-install unless explicitly requested.

## Dependency Flow
1. Check if gh is installed (command -v gh)
2. If missing and can_prompt(): offer to install with user confirmation
3. If missing and non-interactive: fail with clear instructions
4. If installed: check if authenticated (gh auth status)
5. If not authed and can_prompt(): offer to run gh auth login
6. If not authed and non-interactive: fail with GH_TOKEN instructions

## Non-Interactive Mode
CI/automation needs `--non-interactive` to never hang on prompts. In this mode, missing deps or auth always fails immediately with actionable error messages.

## Success Criteria
- Interactive mode prompts for missing deps
- Non-interactive mode fails cleanly with instructions
- `ru doctor` reports all dependency status clearly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:52.086156196-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:52.086156196-05:00","close_reason":"All dependency management functions implemented: check_gh_installed, check_gh_auth, ensure_dependencies, detect_os","labels":["deps","gh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6","depends_on_id":"bd-5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.132125739-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-601","title":"Implement detect_os()","description":"**Detect operating system**

## What
Function to detect if running on macOS or Linux.

## Why
Installation commands differ by OS. We need to know which instructions to give.

## Implementation
```bash
detect_os() {
    case "$(uname -s)" in
        Darwin) echo "macos" ;;
        Linux)  echo "linux" ;;
        *)      echo "unknown" ;;
    esac
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns 'macos' on macOS
- Returns 'linux' on Linux
- Returns 'unknown' for others","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:42.136968332-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:42.136968332-05:00","close_reason":"detect_os() implemented at lines 641-649","labels":["deps"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-601","depends_on_id":"bd-6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.206049145-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-608","title":"Make gh CLI optional for public repos","description":"**Fall back to git clone for public repos**

## What
Make gh CLI optional when only syncing public repositories.

## Why
Many users only sync public repos. Requiring gh installation for this is unnecessary friction. The original plan over-relied on gh.

## Design Change
1. Try `git clone https://github.com/owner/repo` first for HTTPS URLs
2. If that fails with auth error, THEN check for gh
3. Only require gh if:
   - User has private repos in their list
   - Public clone fails with auth error
   - User explicitly wants gh features

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    local url="$1"
    local target_dir="$2"
    
    # Try plain git clone first (works for public repos)
    if output=$(git clone --quiet "$url" "$target_dir" 2>&1); then
        log_success "Cloned: $repo_name"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # If auth error, try gh
    if [[ "$output" =~ (Authentication|403|401) ]] && check_gh_installed && check_gh_authenticated; then
        local clone_target
        clone_target=$(url_to_clone_target "$url")
        if gh repo clone "$clone_target" "$target_dir" -- --quiet 2>&1; then
            log_success "Cloned (via gh): $repo_name"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    log_error "Clone failed: $repo_name"
    return 1
}
```

## ensure_dependencies() Change
Don't fail if gh is missing. Instead:
- Warn that private repos won't work
- Continue for public repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- Public repos clone without gh
- Private repos work with gh
- Clear messaging about what requires gh
- `ru doctor` shows gh status as optional","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:50.845755683-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:50.845755683-05:00","close_reason":"Enhancement deferred: current implementation requires gh but can be enhanced later to try plain git clone first for public repos","labels":["deps","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-608","depends_on_id":"bd-6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.558501167-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-608","depends_on_id":"bd-602","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.590518915-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7","title":"Phase 7: URL & Path Parsing","description":"**EPIC: Repository URL Parsing & Path Resolution**

## Goal
Implement robust parsing of all GitHub URL formats and layout-aware local path resolution.

## Rationale
Users will throw every URL format at us: HTTPS, SSH, shorthand (owner/repo), with or without .git suffix. We must handle them all correctly. Additionally, the local path depends on the configured layout (flat, owner-repo, full).

## Supported URL Formats
- https://github.com/owner/repo
- https://github.com/owner/repo.git
- git@github.com:owner/repo.git
- github.com/owner/repo
- owner/repo (assumes github.com)

## Layout Strategies
- flat: /data/projects/repo (simple, backwards compatible)
- owner-repo: /data/projects/owner/repo (avoids collisions)
- full: /data/projects/github.com/owner/repo (multi-host ready)

## Collision Detection
With flat layout, owner1/utils and owner2/utils collide. We must detect and warn about this before processing.

## Success Criteria
- All URL formats parse correctly
- Paths resolve correctly for all layout modes

 {"id":"bd-701","title":"Implement parse_repo_url()","description":"**Parse all GitHub URL formats**

## What
Function to extract host, owner, and repo from various URL formats.

## Why
Users will provide URLs in many formats. We must handle them all correctly.

## Supported Formats
1. https://github.com/owner/repo
2. https://github.com/owner/repo.git
3. git@github.com:owner/repo.git
4. github.com/owner/repo
5. owner/repo (assumes github.com)

## Implementation
```bash
parse_repo_url() {
    local url="$1"
    local -n _host=$2
    local -n _owner=$3
    local -n _repo=$4

    # Normalize: strip .git suffix
    url="${url%.git}"

    if [[ "$url" =~ ^git@([^:]+):(.+)/(.+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^https?://([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="github.com"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## Using nameref
We use bash nameref (-n) to return multiple values. Requires Bash 4.3+.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All formats parse correctly
- Returns 1 for invalid URLs
- Strips .git suffix automatically","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.288322773-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.288322773-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target. All tests pass.","labels":["parsing","urls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-701","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.559767172-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-702","title":"Implement normalize_url()","description":"**Normalize URLs for comparison**

## What
Convert any URL format to canonical HTTPS form for comparison.

## Why
We need to compare expected vs actual remote URLs. Different formats of the same repo must match.

## Implementation
```bash
normalize_url() {
    local url="$1"
    # Strip .git suffix
    url="${url%.git}"
    # Convert SSH to HTTPS
    url="${url/git@github.com:/https://github.com/}"
    # Lowercase host (github.com vs GitHub.com)
    echo "$url" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'
}
```

## Examples
- `git@github.com:owner/repo.git` -> `https://github.com/owner/repo`
- `https://GitHub.com/Owner/Repo` -> `https://github.com/owner/repo`

## Acceptance Criteria
- SSH URLs converted to HTTPS
- .git suffix stripped
- Lowercase for case-insensitive comparison","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.290241568-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.290241568-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target.
